behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode is at local handoff boundaries (HTTP handler -> signature verifier -> queue publisher). Unit tests on verifier logic alone can miss wiring mistakes where invalid requests still reach enqueue path. Rejected System: behavior text requires `401` and no forwarding, but does not explicitly require real network stack behavior beyond app-internal request/response handling.
behaviors.test_description: Run the handler with mismatched raw body/signature through the app stack (router + middleware + handler + queue adapter) using an in-process test server; assert status 401 and assert queue adapter received no enqueue call.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This requires proving ordering of an externally visible side effect across boundaries: persistence write must complete before sender receives 200. That is SYSTEM TRIAD #3 (round-trip side-effect visibility). Integration fakes can hide flush/commit timing differences.
behaviors.test_description: Start real service with real persistent queue backend; send webhook; capture response completion time and then verify persisted event exists via queue readback/log position proving write happened before 200 was observed.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Core risk is async orchestration across local components (ack path decoupled from downstream worker completion). No explicit requirement for real OS/network/subprocess contracts. Rejected System: behavior text only requires non-blocking control flow, which can be validated with delayed local downstream module.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real handler + queue + downstream worker interface in-process; force downstream processing promise to resolve after a delay; assert HTTP response is sent before downstream completion signal.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: Explicit wall-clock SLO under concurrency depends on real runtime scheduling, I/O contention, and request handling under load; unit/integration fakes can mask timing regressions. This is externally visible response-time behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run service process and issue concurrent requests while prior events trigger intentionally slow downstream processing; measure real wall-clock response latency and assert each ack returns within 3 seconds.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Dedup correctness is a local multi-module handoff problem (ID extraction -> dedup store/check -> enqueue decision). Unit tests of dedup function alone miss wiring/transaction ordering mistakes. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real network/subprocess or external contract beyond local queue write count.
behaviors.test_description: With app stack wired to real dedup storage abstraction and queue adapter, send same event ID twice and assert exactly one persisted/enqueued record.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires external error response contract (`503` + `Retry-After`) conditioned on queue capacity state, matching SYSTEM TRIAD #2 (real external error-path contract). Mock-only tests can miss real header/status propagation at HTTP boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Run service over real HTTP with queue configured to full state; submit webhook and assert actual response status is 503 and `Retry-After` header is present with expected value format.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: Risk is within queue module + persistence backend interaction (capacity accounting + reject path). Requires more than unit logic but can be validated with local real DB/filesystem integration; no explicit external HTTP/subprocess contract required. Rejected System: behavior text focuses on queue-internal enforcement, not an external response contract.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate queue against real local persistence backend with small configured capacity; enqueue until limit, then enqueue one more and assert deterministic rejection and unchanged persisted count.
